rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture when adding an annex; do not alter component structure, features, subcomponents, connections, or properties.
Respect owner and scope: merge contract-style annex content into the component type, and keep implementation-level bindings only in implementation scope.
Use the existing model as the authority for legal owners and scopes; do not invent owners or cross-scope references during fusion.
generic_rules: Attach new content only to a valid existing component or implementation owner.
Preserve all original declarations and formatting as much as possible; make the smallest structural change needed to add the annex.
Do not move logic across type and implementation scopes during insertion.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-scope content should appear after relevant structural declarations when that scope is used.
anti_patterns: Do not insert a type-style contract into an implementation owner or vice versa.
Do not modify ports, connections, subcomponents, or property associations while fusing annex content.
irrelevant_topics: Rules for choosing visible identifiers inside expressions
